title: [Bug]: Play Next File missing in linux
author: gridplayerrocks
body:
### GridPlayer version

v0.5.4

### What OS are you using?

Linux

### OS Version / Linux distribution

Linux Mint Debian edition

### What distribution channel are you using? [LINUX ONLY]

None

### Bug description

Dear Sir

I love Gridplayer!  I have noticed that version v0.5.4 is missing a function that v0.5.3 had.  With version v0.5.4 under Playback the option to Play Next File/Play Previous File with Page Up or Down is still there.  But if you save the Playlist, the next time you open that playlist the function to Play Next File or Previous File is not there.  With version v0.5.3 it remained when the playlist was saved.  I'm using the same distro with both versions.

If updating v0.5.4 is not an option please provide directions for installing a full version of Gridplayer v0.5.3.  The appimage seems to freeze frequently.

If making a donation by mail is an option please let me know.  I’m always reluctant to provide credit card numbers.  I will definitely follow up.

Thank you



### Log excerpt

```shell

```

title: [Bug]: Crash on live HLS stream when overlay formats invalid playback timestamp
author: jeremygold02
body:
### GridPlayer version

0.5.3

### What OS are you using?

Windows

### OS Version / Linux distribution

Windows 11 25H2

### What distribution channel are you using? [LINUX ONLY]

None

### Bug description

GridPlayer crashed while opening multiple live HLS streams. I had 4 streams open/starting, and the 4th stream was struggling to load for a while before the traceback below appeared.

The affected process was launched with a live HLS URL. There were no obvious Streamlink, yt-dlp, VLC, 403/404, or “no segments” errors immediately before this log entry.

It looks like video_overlay.py::set_position may be calling get_time_txt(...) before guarding against invalid/unknown live-stream duration or position values, causing time.gmtime(...) in time_txt.py to receive a value that Windows/Python treats as invalid.

### Log excerpt

```shell
2026-06-06 16:10:29,167 (21184) | UNHANDLED | critical | Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "gridplayer\widgets\video_overlay.py", line 172, in set_position
  File "gridplayer\utils\time_txt.py", line 21, in get_time_txt
  File "gridplayer\utils\time_txt.py", line 38, in _fmt_time
OSError: [Errno 22] Invalid argument
```

title: [Feature request]: Add "Snapshot All" function to capture all video frames at once
author: ShuangQiu
body:
### Suggestion

Currently, GridPlayer allows taking snapshots of individual video windows via the right-click menu. However, when I need to capture the current frame of all playing videos simultaneously (e.g., for comparison or archiving), I have to right-click and save each video one by one. This process is tedious and inefficient, especially when using larger grids. I would like to request a feature to capture snapshots of all active video players at once, either through a "Snapshot All" option in the menu or a global hotkey (e.g., Ctrl + Shift + S). The saved images can follow the existing naming convention, ideally appending the video index to distinguish them. This feature would be extremely useful for users who use GridPlayer for video comparison and multi-angle analysis. Thank you for this amazing open-source tool

title: [Feature request]: suggestion for improvement
author: ronangonidec-ops
body:
### Suggestion

Hello, this is a very good application.

However, there isn't enough freedom in terms of placement.

What would be really nice is if, for example, you could select several videos in the top left corner to loop them, and do the same in the top right corner, and so on.

Regards

title: [Bug]: variable frame rate video playing frame by frame error
author: wczy1219
body:
### GridPlayer version

0.5.4

### What OS are you using?

Windows

### OS Version / Linux distribution

Windows 10

### What distribution channel are you using? [LINUX ONLY]

None

### Bug description

When playing a variable frame rate video (e.g., a 20-second video where 0–10s has a frame rate of 5 and 10–20s has a frame rate of 25), pausing and stepping frame by frame doesn’t work accurately. This is because the code switches frames based on the average frame rate.

However, VLC supports true frame-by-frame switching via its step function. Could GridPlayer also support this? Does VLC provide an interface to enable this feature?

### Log excerpt

```shell

```

title: [Feature request]: In‑App Playlist / Preset Selector UI
author: RustoMCSpit
body:
### Suggestion


Add an in‑app playlist/preset selector UI for quickly loading saved playlists

Gridplayer could have a playlist selection interface within the app. Currently, playlists store all settings (videos, layout, volume, loops, etc.) and function like presets, but opening them requires manually loading the file from disk each time. An integrated UI for browsing and selecting saved playlists/presets would make workflows much smoother, especially for users who save multiple setups.

Proposed functionality:
A playlist manager view accessible from the main menu or sidebar
Listing all saved playlists with filenames and optionally thumbnails or descriptions
Quick load with one click

Benefits:
Saves time re‑opening setups from file manager
